summary.intent: Implement a redacted serialization path for channel config API responses so that secret fields (tokens, passwords) return '[REDACTED]' instead of raw values when serialized for the frontend API.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented per-crate `RedactedConfig` newtypes with manual `Serialize` impls in all four channel crates (Discord, Telegram, Slack, MS Teams), shared secret serde helpers in `moltis-common`, and updated `account_config_json()` in each plugin. All 269 tests pass, fmt and clippy clean.

repo: All four channel crates (discord, telegram, slack, msteams) already depend on `moltis-common`, so no new Cargo.toml dependency additions were needed for the common crate import.
Each channel config previously had local duplicate `serialize_secret`/`serialize_option_secret` helper functions — now consolidated into `moltis_common::secret_serde`.
The project enforces `clippy::unwrap_used` — test modules need `#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]` or must use `.unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!(...))` pattern.
The workspace uses `cargo +nightly-2025-11-30` for fmt and clippy.
WhatsApp channel has no secret fields and required no changes.
There is a pre-existing unrelated build error (missing CSS file) in the workspace that does not affect the channel crates.

code.finding: New shared module providing serialize_secret, serialize_option_secret (storage/raw), serialize_secret_redacted, serialize_option_secret_redacted (API/redacted), and a REDACTED constant.

workflow: Rust's orphan rule prevents implementing a foreign trait (Serialize) for a generic wrapper type (Redacted<T>) when T is defined in a downstream crate — per-crate local newtypes are required instead of a shared generic wrapper.

serde_json::to_value() takes ownership or a reference; passing &RedactedConfig(...) produces a needless borrow warning since the newtype wraps a reference already — pass RedactedConfig(&s.config) directly.
ExposeSecret trait imports used only in #[cfg(test)] blocks should be placed inside the test module rather than at the module root to avoid unused import warnings in non-test builds.

friction: Initial design used a generic `Redacted<T>` wrapper in moltis-common, but Rust's orphan rule made it impossible to impl Serialize for it in downstream crates — required a full redesign to per-crate local newtypes after a failed compile.
Clippy's `needless_borrow` lint flagged `serde_json::to_value(&RedactedConfig(...))` in all plugin files — required a second pass of edits across 4 plugin files and 4 config test files.
ExposeSecret import placement caused potential unused-import warnings; required moving the import into test modules for telegram and slack config files.
open_items: config, oauth, voice, and mcp crates also have duplicated serialize_secret/serialize_option_secret helpers — consolidation into moltis_common::secret_serde is deferred to a follow-up issue.

Manual verification steps remain: start server, hit channel status API endpoint to confirm token fields show '[REDACTED]', and verify channel config round-trips correctly (stop/start account, check secrets persist).
